annotations.decision: history of non-small-cell lung cancer with known chest wall METS
The patient has a history of Horner's x 1 month
In no acute distress
Cardiovascular with a regular rate and rhythm.
Lungs are clear to auscultation bilaterally
Abdomen is soft, nontender, and nondistended

Neuro reveals awake, alert, and oriented x3 with positive dysarthria. Naming is intact. Repetition is intact. No pronator drift. Profound dysmetria with the right finger-to-nose. Left finger-to-nose intact
Right ptosis. Right pupil is 2 down to 1.5 mm. Left is 4 down to 2 mm
Other cranial nerves are intact. Strength on the right side is [**5-8**] in all muscle groups. On the left he is 4+ in the upper extremities and 5 in the lower extremities
Admitted to the ICU
head CT showed a cerebellar hemorrhage with effacement of the 4th ventricle. No hydrocephalus
he remained neurologically intact
transferred to the regular floor
